failure_surface: Task validation failed (test_case_results: [0]) despite agent claiming successful completion. Multiple SyntaxError exceptions occurred in notebook execution due to bare string literals being interpreted as code.
root_cause: Agent attempted to print status summaries as raw text strings outside of print() function calls, causing immediate execution failures. Additionally, there is evidence that the Excel file modifications may not have been properly persisted or validated before reporting completion.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Wrap all status/reporting output in proper print() function calls, and add explicit verification step that confirms the workbook file exists and contains expected changes before declaring task complete.
evidence: Multiple SyntaxError messages showing bare text strings like 'Task finished successfully...' executed as code rather than wrapped in print()
Evaluation result shows test_case_results: [0] indicating validation tests failed despite agent's success claim
Conversation tail repeats the same bare string output three times with identical SyntaxError patterns
Agent claimed operations completed (63 data rows, formulas preserved) but no verification of actual file state was demonstrated

memory_items.title: Proper Status Reporting Syntax
memory_items.lesson: All console output must use print() function; bare string literals cause SyntaxError in code execution environments
memory_items.generalization: When generating executable code, always wrap informational messages in print() or logging calls rather than leaving as standalone strings

memory_items.title: Workbook Persistence Verification
memory_items.lesson: File modifications must be explicitly verified after saving to ensure changes persisted correctly
memory_items.generalization: After writing Excel files, verify file existence, row/column counts, and critical cell values match expected outcomes before reporting task completion

memory_items.title: Validation Before Finalization
memory_items.lesson: Do not declare task success until validation criteria confirm expected outputs exist
memory_items.generalization: Implement pre-commit validation checks against task requirements (row counts, column values, sheet structure) to catch errors before reporting completion

edits.content:
## Error Prevention Best Practices

### Code Execution Hygiene
- All console output must use print() or logging functions; bare string literals will cause SyntaxError when executed
- Never leave raw text strings in executable code blocks without proper function wrapping

### Workbook Validation Protocol
- After saving Excel files, perform verification checks:
  - Confirm file exists at target path
  - Validate row/column counts match expected outcomes
  - Spot-check critical cells contain correct values
  - Ensure no unexpected formatting loss or formula corruption

### Completion Criteria
- Only report task success after validation passes
- Include verification results in output for audit trail
- If any validation fails, log specific errors and halt rather than continuing
